symbols: virgin figure — purity, the Church, or allegorical virtue
description: A Flemish panel painting by Memling showing an allegorical scene with a virgin figure, likely representing a theological virtue or the Church. The composition features characteristic Memling detail and serene facial expression.
theological_description: Allegorical virgins in medieval art often represent the Church as the Bride of Christ (EPH.5.25-27), or theological virtues such as Faith, Hope, and Charity. The virgin imagery draws on the parable of the wise and foolish virgins (MAT.25.1-13) and Paul's description of presenting the Church 'as a pure virgin to Christ' (2CO.11.2).

scripture_refs.reason: The parable of the wise virgins
Presenting the Church as a pure virgin to Christ
Christ and the Church as bridegroom and bride
